title: Droplet dispensing device and system
abstract: A device (6) for providing a droplet of a liquid stored in an interior (51) of a vial (5), comprises a support body (2) and a resilient dome portion (3) tightly mounted to the support body (2) to form a chamber (21) comprising air. The dome portion (3) is configured to create an air flow with the air in the chamber (21) when activated by compression. The support body (2) comprises a vial seat (24) arranged to receive the vial (5), a nozzle (26), an air flow conduit (22) and a transfer conduit (23) connecting the vial seat (24) with the nozzle (26). The support body (2) is arranged that, when the vial (5) is received by the vial seat (24), (i) the air flow conduit (22) establishes an air connection between the interior (51) of the vial (5) and the chamber (21), (ii) the transfer conduit (23) establishes a fluid connection between the vial (5) and the nozzle (26), and (iii) on activation of the dome portion (3), the air flow delivers air into the vial (5) such that a pressure rise is created in the interior (51) of the vial (5) which causes liquid to be transferred from the vial (5) to the nozzle (26) through the transfer conduit (23).
claims:
Claims CLAI MS Claim 1 : Device (6; 60) for providing a droplet of a liquid stored in an interior (51 ; 510) of a vial (5; 50), comprising a support body (2; 20); and a resilient dome portion (3; 30) mounted to the support body (2; 20) to form a chamber (21 ; 210) comprising air, and configured to create an air flow with the air in the chamber (21 ; 210) when activated by compression, wherein the support body (2; 20) comprises a vial seat (24; 240) arranged to receive the vial (5; 50), a nozzle (26; 260), an air flow conduit (22; 220) and a transfer conduit (23; 230) connecting the vial seat (24; 240) with the nozzle (26; 260), the support body (2; 20) is arranged that, when the vial (5; 50) is received by the vial seat (24; 240), the air flow conduit (22; 220) establishes a fluid connection between the interior (51 ; 510) of the vial (5; 50) and the chamber (21 ; 210), the transfer conduit (23; 230) establishes a fluid connection between the vial (5; 50) and the nozzle (26; 260), and on activation of the dome portion (3; 30), the air flow delivers air into the vial (5; 50) such that a pressure rise is created in the interior (51 ; 510) of the vial (5; 50) which causes liquid to be transferred from the vial (5; 50) to the nozzle (26; 260) through the transfer conduit (23; 230). Claim 2: The device (6; 60) of claim 1 , wherein the dome portion (3; 30) is made of a flexible elastomeric material such as silicone. Claim 3: The device (6; 60) of claim 1 or 2, wherein the air flow conduit (22; 220) comprises an end section (251 ; 2510) embodied as puncturing member protruding the vial seat (24; 240) and arranged to pierce a cover of the vial (5; 50) to establish 

 a flow pathway between the chamber (21 ; 210) and the interior (51 ; 510) of the vial (5; 50). Claim 4: The device (6; 60) of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the transfer conduit (23; 230) comprises an end section (252; 2520) embodied as puncturing member protruding the vial seat (24; 240) and arranged to pierce a cover of the vial (5; 50) to establish a flow pathway between the nozzle (26; 260) and the interior (51 ; 510) of the vial (5; 50). Claim 5: The device (6; 60) of claim 3 and 4, wherein the puncturing member of the air flow conduit (22; 220) protrudes the puncturing member of the transfer conduit (23; 230). Claim 6: The device (6; 60) of claim 4 or 5, wherein the puncturing member of the transfer conduit (23; 230) is dimensioned to end in, or adjacent to, the cover of the vial (5; 50), when the vial (5; 50) is received by the vial seat. Claim 7: The device (6; 60) of claim 5 or 6, wherein the puncturing member of the air flow conduit (22; 220) is dimensioned to end outside the liquid in the interior (51 ; 510) of the vial (5; 50), when the vial (5; 50) is received by the vial seat (24; 240). Claim 8: The device (6; 60) of any one of the preceding claims, comprising an air-permeable and liquid tight filter member (27) separating the chamber (21 ; 210) from the air flow conduit (22; 220). Claim 9: The device (6; 60) of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the dome portion (3; 30) is not mounted tightly on the support body (2; 20) to create an air flow pathway to an exterior of the device (6; 60). Claim 10: The device (6; 60) of claim 1 1 , comprising a one-way valve arranged to prevent liquid to be delivered from the transfer conduit (23; 230) through the air duct. 

 Claim 1 1 : The device (6; 60) of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the nozzle (26; 260) of the support body (2; 20) comprises a socket (261 ; 2610) and an exchangeable nozzle insert (262; 2620). Claim 12: The device (6; 60) of claim 13, wherein the nozzle (26; 260) of the support body (2; 20) comprises a seal positioned between the socket (261 ; 2610) and the nozzle insert (262; 2620). Claim 13: The device (6; 60) of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the nozzle (26; 260) comprises a droplet duct (263; 2630) with an open end, the droplet duct extending between the transfer conduit (23; 230) and the open end, and having a droplet generation section (264; 2640) of reduced diameter. Claim 14: Droplet dispensing system (1 ; 10) comprising a device (6; 60) of any one of the preceding claims and a vial (5; 50) with an interior (51 ; 510) in which a liquid to be dispensed is arranged.
